python_lowered_to_cpp_consumed_axes.reason: ProductionManager does not directly consume production_plan.*; provider compilation lowers rich production plans into the existing consumed production and tech axes before publish.

central_polling_hook.reason: GameCommander calls ProductionManager, ScoutManager, and CombatCommander every frame, so this is the safest single polling point.

manager_hooks.intended_effect: Bias doctrine-specific queue, facility, addon, unit, expansion, and continuity choices while preserving MicroMachine build execution.
Turns LLM/user intent into a bounded task ticket consumed by existing MicroMachine managers with accepted/executing/refused lifecycle evidence.

manager_hooks.function: BuildingManager::getBuildingLocation(const Building &, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool)
manager_hooks.intended_effect: Turns semantic building placement tasks into a bounded desired-position anchor consumed by BuildingManager, then lets existing SC2 placement validation choose the nearest valid fallback.

Assigns exact requested combat composition from Idle/Backup/Scout/MainAttack into MainAttack, applies route/target intents, and consumes unit roles for bounded micro/ability bias without pulling defense squads or bypassing SC2 ability availability.
Bias attack/hold/retreat posture, explicit scout-scope assignment, and ranged-unit cooldown kiting through RangedManager without bypassing MicroMachine action planning.
Bias scout risk and information freshness while ScoutManager keeps movement control.
Bias repair/gas/worker-defense policies without directly selecting workers.
Suppress repeated identical worker orders inside a bounded frame window without issuing replacement raw unit commands.
Bias combat analysis thresholds used by higher-level fight decisions.
Bias role allocation/regroup behavior while Squad executes its existing micro managers.
Bias semantic attack scope, reinforce radius, contain position, and target ordering without raw unit tags.
